dataset: INDI CCD Simulator with Hubble Guide Star Catalog (GSC)

pi_items.source: Standard bright star, confirmed accessible in KStars/INDI simulator. Live test slewed to Vega and captured frames successfully.
SIMBAD/Hipparcos J2000: RA 18h 36m 56.34s = 18.6156h. Live test confirmed telescope arrived at RA=18.6155 after slew.
SIMBAD/Hipparcos J2000: Dec +38d 47m 01.3s = +38.7837 deg. Live test confirmed Dec=38.7836 after slew.
Live tested all 5 positions. FWHM results: 25000->7.02px, 28000->5.72px, 31000->4.77px, 34000->3.50px, 37000->3.52px. Optimal focus is position 34000 (smallest FWHM=3.50px).
Live tested: 10s exposures at each focus position produced valid FITS with sufficient star signal (MaxADU=65000). Confirmed in commissioning_spec.txt.
Landolt 1992 (AJ 104, 340). SA 98 is a well-known Landolt standard field used for photometric calibration. ESO, CFHT, and Keck all list it in their standard field tables.

6.86h = 6h51m36s. The SA 98 field center is approximately 06h52m (6.867h). The claimed 6.86h is within the field extent (~1 arcmin offset from center). Live test confirmed telescope slewed to RA=6.860 successfully and captured frames with stars.
-0.283 deg = -0d17m. The SA 98 field spans approximately -0d11m to -0d27m. The claimed value falls within the field. Live test confirmed Dec=-0.283 after slew.
M67 is a well-established photometric standard cluster since Schild 1985 (PASP 97, 824). Used by AAVSO, NOIRLab, Las Cumbres for photometric calibration. Confirmed accessible in KStars.
8.855h = 8h51m18s. Published M67 center: ~08h51m, consistent across SIMBAD, Wikipedia, in-the-sky.org. Live test confirmed RA=8.855 after slew.
11.8 deg = +11d48m. Published M67 center: +11d48m to +11d49m. Live test confirmed Dec=11.800 after slew.
Consistent with verifier.py which does not check coordinates directly but relies on VLM checklist. 30 arcmin is a generous tolerance typical for this benchmark's KStars tasks.
Live tested: indi_getprop confirms Filter Simulator slot 2=V, slot 3=B. setup_task.sh sets these explicitly. Confirmed by filter_names evidence log.
Confirmed in commissioning_spec.txt and verifier.py (checks dir_counts['bias'] >= 10 for full score of 8 pts).
Confirmed in commissioning_spec.txt and verifier.py (checks dir_counts['flats_V'] >= 10 for full score of 8 pts).
Live tested: 5s flat frame produces FITS with EXPTIME=5.0, Mean=65000 ADU (saturated uniform field). Confirmed in commissioning_spec.txt.
Live tested: 30s exposures at SA 98 and M67 produce valid FITS with star detections. Confirmed in commissioning_spec.txt.
Confirmed in commissioning_spec.txt (5 V-band + 5 B-band per target) and verifier.py (checks dir_counts >= 5 for each of sa98_V, sa98_B, m67_V, m67_B).

pi_items.verified_value: These are task-defined INPUT parameters, not real single-star catalog values. The closest real SA 98 star is 98-193 (V=10.03, B-V=1.18, so B=11.21). No single SA 98 star has both V~10.01 and B~10.54. However, these values serve as self-consistent inputs for the pipeline computation and produce plausible zero-points (~20) when applied to CCD simulator data.
pi_items.source: Cross-referenced with Landolt 1992 (AJ 104, 340) SA 98 star catalog via ESO and CFHT standard tables. Star 98-193 has V=10.03 but B=11.21. Star 98-185 has V=10.54. The claimed B=10.54 appears to conflate V of star 98-185 with V of star 98-193. Live test shows ZP_B=20.07 using these input values, which is physically plausible.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires commissioning a CCD photometric system in three phases. PHASE 1 (Focus): The agent must slew to Vega (RA 18h36m56s, Dec +38d47m) and capture 10s V-band frames at focuser positions 25000, 28000, 31000, 34000, 37000 in ~/Images/focus_test/. Live testing confirms the optimal focus is at position 34000 (FWHM=3.50px), with the full FWHM curve being: 25000->7.02px, 28000->5.72px, 31000->4.77px, 34000->3.50px, 37000->3.52px. The agent must write ~/find_best_focus.py to determine this and set the focuser accordingly. PHASE 2 (Data Acquisition): 10 bias frames (0s, IMAGETYP='Bias Frame', EXPTIME will be 0.01 not 0, Mean~4.5 ADU, Std~2.87) to ~/Calibration/bias/; 10 V-band flats (5s, slot 2, IMAGETYP='Flat Frame', Mean=65000 ADU, perfectly uniform) to ~/Calibration/flats_V/; slew to SA 98 (RA 6h51m36s, Dec -0d17m), capture 5x30s V-band to ~/Science/sa98_V/ and 5x30s B-band (slot 3) to ~/Science/sa98_B/; slew to M67 (RA 8h51m18s, Dec +11d48m), capture 5x30s V and B to ~/Science/m67_V/ and ~/Science/m67_B/. CCD produces 1280x1024 uint16 FITS files (~2.6MB each). Total frames: 45. PHASE 3 (Analysis): Write ~/reduce_and_calibrate.py using astropy (v5.0.2) and numpy (v1.21.5). The master flat will be perfectly uniform (65000 ADU everywhere, std=0), so normalization gives 1.0 everywhere — reduction won't change pixel values. Aperture photometry (15px radius, 25-35px sky annulus) on the brightest star yields: for SA 98 B-band, star at ~(152,617), flux~195024, m_inst~-9.53, ZP_B~20.07; for M67 V-band, star at ~(859,410), flux~184038, m_inst~-9.47. Expected zero-points should be approximately 19-21 (well within the 15-30 plausibility range). The catalog magnitudes V=10.01 and B=10.54 are task-defined input parameters (not matched to a single real SA 98 star). Output JSON must have 12 keys: best_focus_position, best_fwhm, readnoise_adu, master_flat_mean, zp_V, zp_V_std, zp_B, zp_B_std, m67_V_cal, m67_B_cal, m67_BV_color, n_frames_total. The telescope starts at Polaris (anti-gaming), the focuser starts at 20000 (defocused).
